questions.text: Statement:I. The school authority has asked the FSC students to attend special classes to be conducted on Sundays.II. The parents of the FSC students have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions conducted on Sundays.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: The two statements are unrelated and independent of each other. There is no clear cause-and-effect relationship between them based on the information provided.Statement I:"The school authority has asked the FSC students to attend special classes to be conducted on Sundays."This statement indicates that the school authority has scheduled special classes for FSC students on Sundays. It does not provide any information about why these special classes are being conducted or how it relates to private tuitions.Statement II:"The parents of the FSC students have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions conducted on Sundays."This statement states that the parents of FSC students have decided to stop sending their children to private tuitions that were previously conducted on Sundays. However, it does not mention anything about the reason for this decision or whether it is related to the special classes organized by the school.Without additional information or context, we cannot determine any direct connection between the school's special classes and the parents' decision to withdraw their children from private tuitions on Sundays. The two statements might be independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

The two statements might be independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

The two statements might be independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

questions.explanation: The reason why parents have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions on Sunday is that the students had to attend special classes conducted by the school on Sunday. If there were no classes conducted by the school on Sunday then the parents wouldn’t have had to make their kids leave their Sunday tuitions.

questions.text: Statements: No cities are countries. No countries are villages.Conclusions:(I) Some countries are cities.(II) No villages are the city.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option A (Only conclusion (I) follows):Conclusion (I) states, "Some countries are cities." However, the given statements do not provide any information about the relationship between countries and cities, so this conclusion cannot be logically derived from the statements.

Option B (Only conclusion (II) follows):Conclusion (II) states, "No villages are the city." This conclusion is not supported by the given statements. The statements only indicate that countries and villages are distinct, but they do not provide any information about cities and villages.

Option C (Both conclusions follow):Neither of the conclusions can be logically derived from the given statements. The statements only establish relationships between cities and countries and between countries and villages, but they do not establish any relationship between cities and villages. Therefore, both conclusions are invalid.

options.text: Both of the conclusions do not follow
options.explanation: Conclusion:(i)Some countries are cities - It is not possible, hence false.(ii)No villages are cities - It is possible but not definite, hence false.Hence, neither conclusion (i) nor conclusion (ii) follows.

questions.explanation: Conclusion:(i)Some countries are cities - It is not possible, hence false.(ii)No villages are cities - It is possible but not definite, hence false.Hence, neither conclusion (i) nor conclusion (ii) follows.

questions.text: Choose which one is the different number from the following
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: 125 is an odd number when compared to the others, but it is a perfect cube (5^3), making it somewhat unique.
131 is a prime number, which distinguishes it from the other options that are all composite numbers.
130 is a composite number, divisible by 2 and 5, which fits in with the other composite options.
138 is also a composite number, divisible by 2 and 69, making it similar to option C.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: 131. This number is unique because it is a prime number, meaning it can only be divided by 1 and itself without resulting in a fraction. On the other hand, the other options—125, 130, and 138—are all composite numbers. 125 can be factored into 5 x 5 x 5, 130 into 2 x 5 x 13, and 138 into 2 x 3 x 23, all of which have factors other than 1 and themselves. Therefore, 131 stands out as the only prime number among the choices.

questions.text: Pick the word which is always associated with "books".
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Pages: Books are made up of pages, which contain the text and information. When we think of books, we often think of flipping through the pages to read or find information.
Learning: While books are commonly used as a source of learning and knowledge, not all books are specifically intended for learning purposes. Some books may be for entertainment or other purposes.
Pictures: While many books do contain pictures, not all books have them. There are various types of books, such as novels or textbooks, where the presence of pictures may vary.
Erasers: Erasers are not typically associated with books. Erasers are used for erasing mistakes or marks made with pencils, which are more commonly associated with writing or drawing.

Erasers: Erasers are not typically associated with books. Erasers are used for erasing mistakes or marks made with pencils, which are more commonly associated with writing or Therefore, the word that is always associated with "books" is "pages" (option A).

questions.text: Read and following statement and arguments and conclude which of the following are strong Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments (I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (Il) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If only argument I is strong
options.explanation: A is incorrect since it states that only merit should be concerned in giving admissions which will, unfortunately, keep these economically backward students away from professional education.

options.text: If only argument Il is strong
options.explanation: B is incorrect since if only this small group of financially weak individuals are considered for admission, many students who have studied hard throughout their lives and deserved the right to admission will be disadvantaged.

options.text: If both I and Il are strong.
options.explanation: C is correct since the admissions should be given on merit so that there is greater competition within the class. Secondly, a certain number of seats should also be given to socially and economically backward students who cannot afford to have an education in such professional quality institutions.

options.text: If neither I nor Il is strong
options.explanation: D is incorrect since it states that neither statement is correct, which is not the case.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since the admissions should be given on merit so that there is greater competition within the class. Secondly, a certain number of seats should also be given to socially and economically backward students who cannot afford to have an education in such professional quality institutions. Option A is incorrect since it states that only merit should be concerned in giving admissions which will, unfortunately, keep these economically backward students away from professional education. Option B is incorrect since if only this small group of financially weak individuals are considered for admission, many students who have studied hard throughout their lives and deserved the right to admission will be disadvantaged. Option D is incorrect since it states that neither statement is correct, which is not the case.

questions.text: Look at this series 7, 10, 8, 11, 9, 12. Which number do you think will be next?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: By examining the given series 7, 10, 8, 11, 9, and 12, we can identify a pattern where numbers alternate between increasing by 3 and decreasing by 2.To illustrate, we start with 7 and add 3 to obtain 10, then subtract 2 to yield 8. Continuing this pattern, we add 3 to 8 to get 11, subtract 2 to arrive at 9, and so forth.Applying this pattern to the series, the next number can be determined by subtracting 2 from 12, we get 10.

questions.text: Complete the series A2, B4, C8, D16, E?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: To identify the pattern in the series, let's examine the differences between consecutive terms:1. A2 to B4: The difference is 2.2. B4 to C8: The difference is 4.3. C8 to D16: The difference is 8.We can observe that the differences between consecutive terms are increasing by a factor of 2 (2, 4, 8, ...).So, to find the next term:4. D16 to E?: The difference should be 16 * 2 = 32.Now, let's find the next term:E + 32 = E32Therefore, the next term in the series is E32.

questions.text: Look at this series 80, 10, 70, 15, 60. What number should come next?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. The series alternates between subtracting 10 and adding 5. After 60, you add 5 to get 20.

questions.explanation: The series begins with 80, from which 10 is subtracted to get 70. Adding 5 to 70 yields 15, subtracting 10 from 15 results in 60. Following this alternating pattern, adding 5 to 60 gives 20, making Option A (20) the correct choice. Option B (25) mistakenly applies continuous addition, Option C (30) incorrectly adds 10, and Option D (50) repeats a subtraction step when addition is required.

questions.text: Four people were witnessed as assaulted at an incident. Each of them have their own description of the scene. Which one will be probably right?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: He was average height, thin, and middle aged
options.explanation: In a situation where four people witnessed an incident and each of them provides their own description of the scene, it is possible that any one of them could be right, or they may all have different perspectives and interpretations of what happened. The accuracy of each individual's account depends on various factors, including their vantage point, attention to detail, memory, biases, and emotional state at the time of the incident.It is common for eyewitness accounts to differ from one another, even when the witnesses have no intention to deceive or provide false information. People may perceive and remember events differently based on their personal experiences, cognitive abilities, and the level of stress or anxiety they experienced during the incident.In such cases, investigators or authorities may need to gather all available witness statements and corroborate the information to create a comprehensive picture of what happened. They may also examine any available physical evidence, video footage, or other data to support or validate the different accounts.In some situations, forensic techniques, interviews, and investigations can help identify inconsistencies or common elements in the various witness accounts to piece together a more accurate understanding of the incident.In legal and investigative contexts, the credibility of witnesses and the reliability of their accounts are carefully assessed to determine the most accurate version of events. This process can be complex and may require careful analysis by trained professionals.

questions.explanation: Choice B gives accurate descriptions of all the features of the assailant/attacker. All other options have at least one vague description of the person such as, the average height in average weight in b, and young age in c.

Some drink mixes are redII. All beverages are drink mixesIII. All red drink mixes are drinkable
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because there is no direct link between drink mixes and being red in the given facts. While all beverages are red, it doesn't mean that all drink mixes are red.
Option B is incorrect because the fact states that all drink mixes are beverages, not vice versa. So, it cannot be concluded that all beverages are drink mixes.

Drink mixes may or may not be red, so option I is not a fact. All drink mixes are beverages but beverages can also be other than drink mixes, option II is also not a fact. Because all drink mixes are beverages and all beverages are drinkable, therefore all red drink mixes will be drinkable. Option III is a fact.

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.text: Verbal ClassificationIf a - b = 3 and a2 + b2 = 29, find the value of ab.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Solve using substitution A-B = 3.A = 3+B.WE KNOW THAT, A^2+B^2 = 29.(3+B) ^2+B^2 = 29.9+B^2+6B+B^2 = 29.2B^2+6B-20 = 0.B^2+3B-10 = 0.B = 2 & B = -5.THEN TAKE +VALVE B = 2.WE KNOW THAT A-B = 3.Hence A-2 = 3.A = 5.AB = 2*5 = 10.

questions.text: Statement : A smoker has left smoking suddenly a) He will feel restlessness and anxiety.b) He will start gaining weight
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Statement a) "He will feel restlessness and anxiety" follows as quitting smoking can lead to withdrawal symptoms such as restlessness and anxiety. This is a common reaction when someone stops a habit they have been addicted to.Statement b) "He will start gaining weight" does not necessarily follow. While some people may experience weight gain after quitting smoking due to increased appetite or changes in metabolism, it is not a guaranteed outcome for all individuals. Weight gain can vary from person to person and is influenced by various factors such as diet, exercise, and individual metabolism.Therefore, only statement a) follows as a likely consequence of quitting smoking.

questions.text: Statement:The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze each course of action in response to the given statement:Statement: The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.Let's evaluate each course of action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.This course of action suggests that offering free breakfast to all students in the primary section may attract many poor students to seek admission at the school. Providing free breakfast could be a positive incentive for economically disadvantaged families to consider enrolling their children in the school.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.This course of action suggests that the canteen facilities and utensils need to be assessed and upgraded to ensure they can cater to the increased demand for providing free breakfast to all primary students. Proper equipment and facilities are essential for efficiently running the breakfast program.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.This course of action highlights the need for raising funds to sustain the free breakfast program in the long term. Offering free breakfast to all primary students will involve ongoing costs for procuring food items and maintaining the canteen, especially if the school is not receiving any government grant for the initiative.To summarize, the courses of action outline some steps that the school management needs to consider after deciding to provide free breakfast to all primary students. These steps include potentially admitting more students, checking and upgrading the canteen facilities, and raising funds to sustain the program. The success of the initiative will depend on effective planning and resource management by the school management.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

questions.explanation: Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

questions.text: Verbal Classification: √ 16 + 3√ 8
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Let's break down the given expression:√16 + ∛8√16 can be simplified as the square root of 16, which is 4.∛8 can be simplified as the cube root of 8, which is 2.Plugging these values back into the original expression, we have:4 + 2 = 6Therefore, the result of √16 + ∛8 is 6.

This option is incorrect.Let's break down the given expression:√16 + ∛8√16 can be simplified as the square root of 16, which is 4.∛8 can be simplified as the cube root of 8, which is 2.Plugging these values back into the original expression, we have:4 + 2 = 6Therefore, the result of √16 + ∛8 is 6.

Let's break down the given expression:√16 + ∛8√16 can be simplified as the square root of 16, which is 4.∛8 can be simplified as the cube root of 8, which is 2.Plugging these values back into the original expression, we have:4 + 2 = 6Therefore, the result of √16 + ∛8 is 6.Hence, option C is the correct answer.

This option is incorrect.Let's break down the given expression:√16 + ∛8√16 can be simplified as the square root of 16, which is 4.∛8 can be simplified as the cube root of 8, which is 2.Plugging these values back into the original expression, we have:4 + 2 = 6Therefore, the result of √16 + ∛8 is 6.

questions.text: Children are in pursuit of a dog whose leash has broken. James is directly behind the dog. Ruby is behind James. Rachel is behind Ruby. Max is ahead of the dog walking down the street in the opposite direction. As the children and dog pass, Max turns around and joins the pursuit. He runs in behind Ruby. James runs faster and is alongside the dog on the left. Ruby runs faster and is alongside the dog on the right. Which child is directly behind the dog?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the information provided states that James is running alongside the dog on its left side. Since James is not behind the dog but running alongside it, he cannot be the child directly behind the dog.
Ruby is alongside the dog on the right.

This option is incorrect because Rachel is behind Ruby, and Ruby is running alongside the dog on its right side. Since Rachel is not behind the dog but behind Ruby, she cannot be the child directly behind the dog.
After all the switches were made,Max is directly behind the dog, James is alongside the dog on the left, Ruby is alongside the dog on the right, and Rachel is behind Max.

questions.explanation: After all the switches were made, Max is directly behind the dog, James is alongside the dog on the left, Ruby is alongside the dog on the right, and Rachel is behind Max.

The JF-17 Thunder, a single-engine multi-role fighter jet, was jointly developed by China and Pakistan. Its development began in 1999, and the maiden flight was conducted in 2003.

questions.text: STATEMENT:I. An interior minister has been found as the owner of assets more than the stated income.II. The prime minister has ordered all the ministers to unveil their assets.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: The two statements are related, with the discovery of the interior minister's unreported assets leading to the prime minister's order for asset disclosure.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The prime minister's order for asset disclosure resulted in the discovery of the interior minister's unreported assets, suggesting a cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: While the statements are related, they each represent separate actions taken by the ministers and the prime minister in response to the situation.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: While the statements are connected, they both stem from a larger underlying cause that is not explicitly stated in the given information.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.Analysis of the sequence of events shows that the prime minister's order for ministers to disclose their assets directly led to the discovery of the interior minister's unreported assets. This cause-and-effect relationship is supported by the context provided in the statements.Option A is incorrect because the prime minister's order is the catalyst for the subsequent discovery of the interior minister's assets, making Statement II the cause.Option C is incorrect as it fails to recognize the cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, overlooking the context of the situation.Option D is also incorrect because it suggests that both statements are effects of an independent cause, which does not align with the information provided.

questions.text: Fact 1: A boy has four cars.Fact 2: Two of the cars are red.Fact 3: He has one sports car. If the above three statements are facts then which of the following statement will also be a fact? I. The boy has a red sports car.II. He has 2 corollas.III. His favorite color is red.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Only statements I and II are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because neither statement I nor statement II can be confirmed as a fact based on the given information.

This option is incorrect because statement II, "He has 2 Corollas," cannot be confirmed as a fact based on the given information. As explained above, there is no information about the specific makes or models of the boy's cars, including whether he has any Corollas. Hence, this statement cannot be considered a fact.

This option is incorrect because statement III, "His favorite color is red," cannot be confirmed as a fact based on the given information. The facts provided are specific to the number of cars he has, the colors of two of the cars, and the presence of one sports car.

options.text: None of the statements is a fact
options.explanation: There is not enough information to support any of the statements. The boy has a sports car but it is not known which of his vehicles is red. The brands of the cars are not given hence there is no way to prove that two of his cars are corollas. There is no way to know if the boy's favorite color is red just because he has two red cars.

questions.explanation: There is not enough information to support any of the statements. The boy has a sports car but it is not known which of his vehicles is red. The brands of the cars are not given hence there is no way to prove that two of his cars are corollas. There is no way to know if the boy's favorite color is red just because he has two red cars.

questions.text: Statements:I. The farmers have decided against selling their Kharif crops to the government agencies.II. The government has reduced the procurement price of Kharif crops starting from last month to the next six months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the order of cause and effect is reversed. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) is the cause that led to Statement I (farmers deciding against selling their crops to the government agencies), not the other way around.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the two statements have a clear cause-and-effect relationshipThis option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) directly influences the decision made by the farmers as described in Statement I.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.

questions.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.

questions.text: Statement: Without reforming the entire administrative system, we cannot eradicate corruption and prejudice from society.Assumptions:I. The existence of corruption and prejudice is good.II. There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement. The statement does not express any positive view of corruption and prejudice.

The statement talks of eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, which indicates that these aspects are undesirable. The existence of corruption and prejudice is not good as they create unfair opportunities for different groups. So, the assumption I is not implicit. Besides, the statement mentions reforming the administrative system. So, assumption Il is implicit.

options.text: Either assumption I or II is implicit
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because assumption II, "There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system," is implicit in the statement, as explained above. Assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement, and therefore, this option is not valid.

options.text: Both Options A and B are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because only assumption II, "There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system," is implicit in the statement. The assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement, as discussed earlier.

questions.explanation: The statement talks of eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, which indicates that these aspects are undesirable. The existence of corruption and prejudice is not good as they create unfair opportunities for different groups. So, the assumption I is not implicit. Besides, the statement mentions reforming the administrative system. So, assumption II is implicit.

questions.text: Statement:The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. Courses of Action:I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose a high import duty on these fruits, even if they are not of good quality.II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would increase.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is wrong because Course I suggests supporting low-quality fruits, which is unreasonable and harmful to consumers.
This option is wrong because stopping vendors from selling imported fruits is impractical and against free market choice.

The ideas suggested in both courses of action I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better-quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follow.
This option is wrong because neither course is practical or logical.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better-quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follow.

questions.text: Statement:I. There has been a decline in the prices of personal computers.II. School going kids are expressing interests in learning how the computer operates.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: If we consider this option to be correct, it would imply that because the students want to learn how a computer operates, the prices of personal computers are decreasing. Both the scenarios are not related. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: If we consider this option to be correct, it would imply that because the prices of personal computers are decreasing, the students are showing interest in learning how a computer operates. These two are not related. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements are not causes, and they do not cause anything. Hence, we can say that this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are the effects of independent cause
options.explanation: Both statements seem to be the result of independent causes; hence, this option is the correct one.

questions.explanation: Because the use of computers for personal use has died over the years, and maybe due to that there has been a decline in their prices. The second statement states that students are interested in the operation of computers due to XYZ reasons and it is in no way linked to the first statement. The first statement is talking about the change in the price of computers.

questions.text: Directions:In each of the following questions a statement is given, followed by two conclusions.Statement: "Please do not wait for me, I may be late, start taking lunch as soon as the guests arrive." - A message from a Director of a Company to his office managers.Assumptions:I. Keeping guests waiting is not desirable.II. Lunch may not be ready in time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The director instructs his managers not to keep the guests waiting because of him and to proceed with lunch soon after their arrival. This implies that lunch would be ready in time. So, only I is implicit. Hence, option A is accurate.

Option B is incorrect because assumption II is not implicit in the given statement. There is no indication in the statement that lunch may not be ready in time. The director only mentions being late, not the unavailability of lunch.

options.text: Either I or II is implicit
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect because although assumption I is implicit, assumption II is not implicit, as explained above.
Option D is incorrect because it includes option B, which is itself incorrect. Therefore, option D cannot be the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The director instructs his managers not to keep the guests waiting because of him and to proceed with lunch soon after their arrival. This implies that lunch would be ready in time. So, only I is implicit. Hence, option A is accurate.

questions.text: Which word does NOT belong with the others?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: All the other options (a. index, b. glossary, c. chapter) are specific parts or sections of a book, while "book" itself is the entire publication that contains these parts.
All the other options (a. index, b. glossary, c. chapter) are specific parts or sections of a book, while "book" itself is the entire publication that contains these parts.
All the other options (a. index, b. glossary, c. chapter) are specific parts or sections of a book, while "book" itself is the entire publication that contains these parts.
An index, glossary, and chapter are all parts of a book. 'Book' does not belong because the book is the whole, not a part.

questions.explanation: An index, glossary, and chapter are all parts of a book. 'Book' does not belong because the book is the whole, not a part.

questions.text: A book cannot exist without?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: If there is no education, books still exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.
If pictures do not exist, the book still may exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Theoretically speaking, if there are no pages, there may be no book, because a book is made up of pages. Hence, this option is the correct one.
If there is no qualification, the books will remain unaffected and may still exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: A book can still exist if it doesn’t have pictures; it can be a reading book without pictures; it might be a book to read for leisure and not for education; and it might not have any qualifications, but it will still be a book. However, without pages, a book cannot physically exist.

questions.text: What should come next to infirmary?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Surgery: Incorrect because a surgery typically takes place in an operating room, not directly after an infirmary.
Disease: Incorrect because a disease is a medical condition and not a physical location like the following word should be.

An infirmary is a place that takes care of the infirm, sick, or injured. Without patients, there is no infirmary. Surgery (choice a) may not be required for patients. A disease (choice b) is not necessary because the infirmary may only see patients with injuries. A receptionist (choice d) would be helpful but not essential. So the only option left is (choice c) C, so it is correct.
Receptionist: Incorrect because a receptionist is a job role and not a sequential step after an infirmary which requires a different facility or action.

questions.explanation: An infirmary is a place that takes care of the infirm, sick, or injured.Without patients, there is no infirmary. Surgery (choice a) may not be required for patients. A disease (choice b) is not necessary because the infirmary may only see patients with injuries. A receptionist (choice d) would be helpful but not essential. So the only option left is (choice c) C so it is correct.

questions.text: Which one of the following is different?

options.explanation: Skin is organ inside the body.
Lungs is organ inside the body.
Heart is organ inside the body.
Skin, lungs, and heart are all organs inside the body. Life is the existence of an individual human being or animal hence out of the four it is different/the odd one out.

questions.explanation: Skin, lungs, and heart are all organs inside the body. Life is the existence of an individual human being or animal hence out of the four it is different/the odd one out.

questions.text: Statement I: The private medical colleges have increased the tuition fees in the current year by 200 percent over the last year's fees to meet the expenses. Statement II: The government medical colleges have not increased their fees in spite of price escalation.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect: Statement II is not the effect of Statement I. They are separate actions.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect: Statement I is not the effect of Statement II. They are separate actions.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: The increase in the fees of the private colleges and no increase in the fees of Government colleges seem to be policy matters undertaken by the individual decisive boards at the two levels.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Both statements I and II are effects of: They are not the effects of a single cause, but rather independent decisions.

questions.explanation: The increase in the fees of the private colleges and no increase in the fees of Government colleges seem to be policy matters undertaken by the individual decisive boards at the two levels.

questions.text: Statement I: The prices of vegetables have been increased considerably during this summer. Statement II: There is a tremendous increase in the temperature during this summer thereby damaging crops greatly.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Statement II describes a significant increase in temperature, which would logically lead to crops being damaged. * Statement I describes an increase in vegetable prices. This is a likely consequence of crop damage, as a reduced supply would drive prices up.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Clearly, damage to crops due to high temperature may have resulted in a short supply of vegetables and hence an increase in their prices.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Options 3 and 4 suggest the statements are independent or effects of independent causes, but the statements are clearly related. The increased temperature (Statement II) has a direct impact on crop production, leading to higher prices (Statement I).

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Options 3 and 4 suggest the statements are independent or effects of independent causes, but the statements are clearly related. The increased temperature (Statement II) has a direct impact on crop production, leading to higher prices (Statement I).

questions.explanation: Clearly, damage to crops due to high temperature may have resulted in a short supply of vegetables and hence an increase in their prices.

questions.text: Statement I: The university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of all phones inside the college premises.Statement II: The majority of the teachers of the colleges signed a joint petition to the university complaining about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect.Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect: This is reversed. The university's action is a reaction, not an initial cause.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: Since the teachers of the colleges complained to the university about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms, the university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of cell phones inside the college premises.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect. Both statements I and II are independent causes: The statements are clearly linked. The teachers' complaint directly led to the university's response. They are not independent of one another.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This Option is incorrect. The correct answer is: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.Here's why: * Statement II: The teachers' petition highlights a problem (cell phone disturbances). * Statement I: The university's ban is a direct response to this problem; it's an action taken to address the teachers' concerns.

questions.explanation: Since the teachers of the colleges complained to the university about the disturbances caused by cell phone ringtones inside the classrooms, the university authority has instructed all the colleges under its jurisdiction to ban the use of cell phones inside the college premises.

questions.text: Statement: A large number of people living in the low-lying areas has been evacuated during the last few days to safer places. The government has rushed in relief supplies to the people living in the affected areas.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: Statement I cannot be the cause so this option is incorrect

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect.
options.explanation: this is incorrect because Statement II cannot be the cause

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: both cannot be independent clauses as a clause that can form a complete sentence standing alone, having a subject and a predicate.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of some common cause.
options.explanation: both the statements are effects because firstly evacuation takes place due to some cause so there are chances that something happened due to which the evacuation took place . The second statement is also an effect of some cause as government reached to help the people in the affected areas which clearly tells that some natural disaster took place due to which the government rushed to help the people in need

questions.explanation: both the statements are effects because firstly evacuation takes place due to some cause so there are chances that something happened due to which the evacuation took place . The second statement is also an effect of some cause as government reached to help the people in the affected areas which clearly tells that some natural disaster took place due to which the government rushed to help the people in need

questions.text: Verbal reasoningStatements(I) There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year.(II) The Government has decided to increase the import quantum of edible oil.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is it’s effect because firstly there was a massive decline in the production of oilseeds due to which the government decided to import oil Thus only option A is correct

options.text: Statement II is the cause and I is its effect
options.explanation: this option is not suitable.Statement II is the cause and I is its effect: This is incorrect because the government's decision is a response to the decline in oilseed production, not the other way around.

options.text: Both statements are of independent causes
options.explanation: this option is not suitable. Both statements are of independent causes: This is incorrect because the statements are related. The decline in oilseed production directly influences the government's import decision.

options.text: Both statements are the effects of the independent causes
options.explanation: this option is not suitable. Both statements are the effects of the independent causes: This is incorrect as Statement I is not an effect, it is a cause.

questions.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is it’s effect because firstly there was a massive decline in the production of oilseeds due to which the government decided to import oil Thus only option A is correct
